title: LEMON BALM
description: Lemon balm (Melissa officinalis) is a perennial herb in the mint family known for its high content of rosmarinic acid, which increases GABA activity by inhibiting the enzyme GABA transaminase. It is primarily used as a mild sedative and cognitive enhancer, functioning as an anxiolytic and acetylcholinesterase inhibitor to promote relaxation and mental clarity.

research.title: Clinical Efficacy and Tolerability of Lemon Balm (Melissa officinalis L.) in Psychological Well-Being: A Review
research.summary: A comprehensive review confirming that lemon balm significantly supports psychological health, particularly in reducing anxiety and improving overall well-being.

research.title: Attenuation of Laboratory-Induced Stress in Humans After Acute Administration of Melissa officinalis (Lemon Balm)
research.summary: This study demonstrated that a 600mg dose of lemon balm reduced laboratory-induced stress and improved self-rated calmness and mood.

research.title: Modulation of Mood and Cognitive Performance Following Acute Administration of Single Doses of Melissa Officinalis
research.summary: Research found that standardized extracts can improve cognitive performance and mood, suggesting potential as an adjunct therapy for neurodegenerative conditions.

sentiment.summary: The majority of users report success using lemon balm for acute anxiety and racing thoughts, often preferring it over stronger sedatives for its 'subtle' but effective calming nature.

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Highly effective when stacked with L-Theanine or Lavender; often cited as more reliable than Valerian root for some.

effects.label: Mild for Chronic Issues:
effects.description: While effective for mild anxiety, users with severe insomnia or cortisol-driven early waking often found it too weak to solve the problem alone.

effects.label: Acute vs Chronic:
effects.description: Users report immediate benefits for stress, but some notice a slight buildup in efficacy over a few weeks for mood stability.

dosage.label: Standard Herbal Tea:
dosage.description: One cup of strong tea before bed is a common entry point for those avoiding extracts.

dosage.label: Standardized Extract:
dosage.description: 300mg to 600mg daily is the common clinical and user range for anxiety management.

dosage.label: Sleep-Specific Dose:
dosage.description: 200mg to 300mg of concentrated extract (like Cyracos) taken 30-60 minutes before bedtime.

dosage.label: Emergency Use:
dosage.description: Some users keep a high-dose tincture for acute panic or high-stress workplace interactions.

sideEffects.label: Morning Groginess:
sideEffects.description: Occasionally reported if taken in very high doses or combined with other strong sedatives.

sideEffects.label: Mild Lethargy:
sideEffects.description: Some users feel 'too chill' or unmotivated if taken in the morning during high-focus work.

sideEffects.label: Dependency Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Rare, but some users worry about long-term GABA-A receptor downregulation, though evidence for this is sparse compared to benzos.

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Found as a common tea, tincture, or encapsulated extract in most health food stores.

availability.label: General Safety:
availability.description: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) and available over-the-counter globally without restriction.
